revision.content:
Satellite Networked Open Ground Station

I'm currently working on getting two types of ground stations working. The first will be a stationary/no-rotator station which can pick up satellites as they pass overhead. The second station will be a rotator based station. This page will track my progress on both stations. Info on the work I did on the v2 rotator ground station can be found here: [[SatNOGS_Ground_Station_v2]] == No-Rotator Station == I have a UHF "paralindy" antenna I've built with cshields, we did some preliminary testing and found they work well enough to continue testing. I've also mostly completed a 2m Lindenblad that the paralindy was loosely based on, that seems to work too. == v3.1 Rotator Station == Piecing together a complete build-of-materials for the latest version of the rotator is a little challenging. As of February 2019 a good summary can be found in this forum post: [https://community.libre.space/t/ground-station-hardaware-report/3235 https://community.libre.space/t/ground-station-hardaware-report/3235]. When looking for documentation it's important to remember that information can live in 3 places: The wiki, the assembly guides, and in gitlab. ====Build page for the hardware/mechanical parts==== * * ====Build page for the rotator controller (electronics)==== * [https://wiki.satnogs.org/SatNOGS_Rotator_Controller https://wiki.satnogs.org/SatNOGS_Rotator_Controller] * BOM for above board: [https://gitlab.com/librespacefoundation/satnogs/satnogs-rotator-controller/blob/v2.2/satnogs-rotator-controller-bom.ods https://gitlab.com/librespacefoundation/satnogs/satnogs-rotator-controller/blob/v2.2/satnogs-rotator-controller-bom.ods] * The position encoder needed for DC motors is a separate project: [https://gitlab.com/librespacefoundation/satnogs/satnogs-rotator-controller-encoderhttps://gitlab.com/librespacefoundation/satnogs/satnogs-rotator-controller-encoder] * ==== ==== * == Also See == * [[SatNOGS_no-rotator_Ground_Station]] [[Category:Dosman projects]] [[Category:Radio]]
